Legend Origins

Archive Records

  • Why Names Have Power in Legends: True Names, Summoning, and the Risk of Being Known
  • Why Circles Protect in Folklore: Boundaries, Ritual Space, and the Shape That Keeps Danger Out
  • Why Crossroads Become Supernatural Places: Choices, Boundaries, and Meetings at the Road’s Edge
  • Why Ravens Become Omens: Black Birds, Battlefield Memory, and the Messenger Bird Motif
  • Why Mirrors Mean Bad Luck: Reflections, Souls, and the Folklore of Broken Glass
  • Rabbit's Foot Luck Origin: The Charm, the Animal, and the Superstition of Carried Luck
  • Evil Eye Origin: How a Harmful Glance Became One of the World's Oldest Protective Beliefs
  • Horseshoe Luck Origin: Iron, Doorways, and How a Stable Object Became a Protective Sign
  • Black Cat Superstition Origin: Luck, Witchcraft, and How an Animal Became an Omen
  • Jack-o-Lantern Origin: Stingy Jack, Turnips, and the Halloween Light
  • Friday the 13th Origin: Superstition, Metafolklore, and the Making of an Unlucky Date
  • The Checklist Screen Catalog Page Kept Outside the Sequence
  • The Signature File Notebook That Adds One Entry Overnight
  • The Map Error Desk Request Slip Signed by No Reader
  • The Guestbook Path Map Note Pointing to a Removed Door
  • The Ledger Shelf Inventory Tag for an Object No One Stored
  • The Census Drawer Audit Log From a Silent Hour
  • The Timestamp Table Receipt Book With a Future Stamp
  • The Receipt Archive Desk Index Card for an Unlisted Shelf
  • The Field Notebook Archive Ledger That Refuses the Final Number
  • The Motif Index Shelf Register With a Line Written Too Early
  • How Census Records Turn Absence Into Story
  • Why Call Sheets Create Voice and Radio Legends
  • How Inventories Make Mythic Objects Feel Real
